semantic.role: Centralized configuration facade for ArchSpine, providing a unified read/write API over project settings with environment overrides and policy integration.
responsibilities: Load and persist the SpineConfig structure from the filesystem via the loader module.
Expose config values for LLM provider settings, artifact strategies, and initialization state.
Provide mutator methods (setLLMValue, clearLLMValue) to modify active configuration with automatic persistence.
Integrate with scan policy resolution and pre-commit hook configuration.
Implement the ConfigSupportedValueAccess interface to support validated configuration keys.
Resolve environment variable overrides (via parseBooleanEnv) with precedence over file values.
Handle config path resolution, existence checks, and file I/O through the FileSystemManager utility.
outOfScope: Direct user authentication or credential management.
AST extraction or source code scanning.
Runtime execution state management or task scheduling.
Direct interaction with LLM providers or API calls.
Multi-language document management or atlas generation.
invariants: Infra consumers must access configuration through this public facade rather than importing sub-modules like loader, env, or supported-values directly.
Config values from environment variables always override values stored in the configuration file.
The Config class must remain the single entry point for all configuration mutations and reads within the infra layer.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a stable, high-level abstraction over the configuration subsystem so that consumers do not depend on low-level file I/O or environment parsing logic, enabling safe refactors of the config internals.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Apply release gate fixes and sync accumulated source changes, ensuring the facade remains consistent with other modules after recent commits.

publicSurface.description: Facade class implementing ConfigSupportedValueAccess. Exposes methods to read and write SpineConfig, manage LLM/artifacts/init-state settings, and integrate with scan policy and pre-commit configuration. All infra-layer configuration access should go through this class.
